_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Selecting and using training/instructional methods and procedures appropriate for the situation when learning or teaching new things.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data literacy program design, including competency assessment, curriculum development, delivery modalities, and measurement of literacy improvement across organizational roles.
comments: Strong overlap: WIDAI LS-K-012 directly covers training/instructional design for data literacy. O*NET is broader (all learning/teaching contexts). WIDAI also covers mentoring (LS-S-007, AB-S-004) which involves instructional skills.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score reflects a genuine but bounded intersection. The overlapping concepts are detectable but represent a subset of what each description covers independently.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8502 indicates lower token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Entailment-dominant (55.39%) — the model detects that one description logically implies aspects of the other. Entailment in an Intersects relationship indicates the overlapping portion carries strong implicational weight — the shared concepts are tightly linked, not loosely associated. The split between entailment and neutral indicates the relationship sits at the boundary — partially implicational, partially independent.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.3236) is moderately lower than the STS cross-encoder (0.4281). The gap (0.10) indicates the relationship has a cross-abstraction component — some of the semantic alignment requires joint processing to detect, suggesting vocabulary or scope divergence between the two descriptions.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Minimal lexical overlap (2.86% of tokens shared). The strong contrast between near-zero lexical overlap and the STS semantic score confirms this is a cross-domain relationship — the concepts are related through meaning, not vocabulary. This is expected for O*NET-to-WIDAI comparisons where general occupational language maps to domain-specific data/AI terminology.
